name: Assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand
summary: Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria was assassinated by Gavrilo Princip, triggering a chain of events that led to the outbreak of World War I.

name: Austria-Hungary Declares War on Serbia
summary: Austria-Hungary declared war on Serbia following the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand, marking the official beginning of World War I.

name: Germany Declares War on France
summary: Germany declared war on France as part of its broader war strategy, expanding the conflict into a major European war.

name: United Kingdom Declares War on Germany
summary: The United Kingdom entered World War I after Germany invaded Belgium, escalating the conflict into a global war.

name: Battle of the Marne
summary: Allied forces halted the German advance toward Paris, marking a turning point in the early stages of World War I.

name: Japan Declares War on Germany
summary: Japan entered World War I on the side of the Allies, seeking to expand its influence in East Asia and the Pacific.

name: Ottoman Empire Enters World War I
summary: The Ottoman Empire joined the Central Powers, opening new fronts in the Middle East and expanding the scope of World War I.

name: First Battle of Ypres
summary: One of the first major battles of World War I, it marked the end of mobile warfare on the Western Front and the beginning of trench warfare.

name: Panama Canal Officially Opens
summary: The Panama Canal was officially opened, revolutionizing global maritime trade by connecting the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans.
